programme.title: CBC News: Morning
programme.desc: What do you need to know about the world around you? Heather Hiscox takes the helm as the anchor for this weekday-morning news program to tell you. Joining her is Colleen Jones with weather and sports info., and Danielle Bochove with all you need to know about the business world.

programme.title: W5
programme.desc: W5 investigates romance scams and reveals a global criminal organization behind the ripoffs and tragic consequences.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Save your abundance of fresh herbs from wilting away with Mary’s recipes to use ‘em up in a jiff.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Celebrate the season with Mary’s recipes that take advantage of limited-edition produce, including tomatoes, zucchini, peppers and berries.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Whether you’re a newbie baker or a pro who wants to take it easy, these recipes are all about supermarket hacks to raise your baking game.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Make your own checkered tablecloth magic with Mary’s recipes for Italian-American restaurant night.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Hosted by chef and two-time Canadian Screen Award-winner Mary Berg, follows Berg as she takes viewers step-by-step through recipes.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Impress pals with recipes that are great to make and easier to take, and they just might steal the show at the next potluck.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Mary shows how to up the baking game without breaking the bank on fancy gadgets. There’s no specialty equipment here!

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Mary keeps shopping lists short with flavourful dishes made up of just a few ingredients.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Celebrate the veg life with Mary’s meatless recipes that are so much more than just a bowl of greens.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Mary shakes up a cocktail of recipes that use beer and wine to load up on flavor.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Mary's veg versions of meaty classics include burgers, nugs and even bacon.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Taking cooking off the to-do list with Mary's batch-made meals to fill the freezer.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Mary Berg takes viewers step-by-step through recipes that solve everyday cooking conundrums.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Making Parmesan corn chowder, twice baked potato casserole and cinnamon oat pancakes with scraps.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Mary Berg takes it step by step through recipes guaranteed to impress without the stress.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Mary Berg takes it step by step through recipes guaranteed to impress without the stress.

programme.title: Mary Makes It Easy
programme.desc: Recipes for shrimp scampi linguini; Mary makes chocolate peanut butter lava cakes.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.sub-title: Thai Food Inspired by Other Cultures
programme.desc: Thai chef Pailin Chongchitnant shows us some Thai dishes that were inspired by food from other cultures.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: It’s all about street food in this Vietnamese episode.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Mary Tang cooks up Cantonese recipes perfect for days when you need comfort food.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Fun host Lisa Nguyen shows us just how versatile noodles are.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Lisa Nguyen shows us recipes from Vietnam's capital city of Hanoi.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Home cook Mary Tang shows us popular Hong Kong culinary delights.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Host Mary Tang shows us how to celebrate the Moon Festival.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Host Mary Tang cooks up traditional Cantonese dim sum recipes.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Host Mary Tang shows us four easy Cantonese seafood recipes.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.sub-title: Vietnamese Food With a French Twist
programme.desc: Vietnamese dishes with French influence.

programme.title: Up The Dish
programme.desc: Up the Dish's four chefs take a shot at dumplings, from classic Americana to upscale European!

programme.title: Up The Dish
programme.desc: Up the Dish's four chefs go raw – and raw vegan – with their variations on a classic Japanese dish.

programme.title: Up The Dish
programme.desc: Up the Dish's four chefs offer worldwide inspirations on a classic rice dish.

programme.title: Up The Dish
programme.desc: Up the Dish's four chefs begin in East Asia with classic Pad Thai then make twists inspired by the classic.

programme.title: CombiNation Plates
programme.desc: Bianca Osbourne combines the cuisines of Italy and China to create inventive dishes.

programme.title: CombiNation Plates
programme.desc: Host Bianca Osbourne combines the cuisines of Georgia and Hong Kong to create delicious dishes.

programme.title: CombiNation Plates
programme.desc: Host Bianca Osbourne combines the cuisines of South Korea and Spain to create inventive dishes.

programme.title: CombiNation Plates
programme.desc: Host Bianca Osbourne celebrates the tastes of Morocco and Thailand and creates delicious dishes combining their cuisines.

programme.title: Spencer's Big 30
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts makes a full, family-style Indian meal in around 30 minutes.

programme.title: Spencer's Big 30
programme.desc: Why order take-out when you can make it fresher, faster, and even healthier at home?

programme.title: Spencer's Big 30
programme.desc: It's Tandoori Take-Out night as host Spencer Watts prepares five quick and easy Indian dishes that take little time, but are big in flavour.

programme.title: Spencer's Big 30
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts prepares a delicious Chinese Take-Out meal at home in just 30 minutes.

programme.title: Spencer's Big 30
programme.desc: Thai food is on the menu as Chef Spencer Watts cooks up an affordable dinner in around 30 minutes.

programme.title: Spencer's Big 30
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts makes fast and affordable Japanese food for four, all in around 30 minutes.

programme.title: Spencer's Big 30
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts makes a fresh and fragrant Vietnamese meal for four for around 30 dollars.

programme.title: Fish The Dish
programme.desc: Spencer spices things up as he makes pepper-inspired dishes with catfish and clams.

programme.title: Fish The Dish
programme.desc: Spencer dresses sablefish in a mole sauce that has a hint of maple and chocolate; lobster has its sweet side.

programme.title: Fish The Dish
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts cooks up fast faves using scallops and trout, all done in under 30 minutes.

programme.title: Fish The Dish
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts mixes the sweet flavors of fruit with fish as he makes dishes with salmon and scallops.

programme.title: Fish The Dish
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts creates some dairy-infused dishes using red snapper and scampi.

programme.title: Fish The Dish
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts creates recipes that combine sour and tart flavors with grouper clams.

programme.title: Fish The Dish
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts demonstrates how a little caffeinated flavour can bring your fish dish to the next level.

programme.title: Fish The Dish
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts shows how you can blend meat and seafood flavours to create tantalizing surf and turf dishes.

programme.title: Fun'Q with Spencer Watts
programme.desc: Bring on the face wiping, finger licking goodness with messy and delicious, fall off the bone, soya beef ribs with sesame mayo.

programme.title: Fun'Q with Spencer Watts
programme.desc: Spencer Watts is inspired to embrace big, big food for a big big barbecue feast.

programme.title: Fun'Q with Spencer Watts
programme.desc: Delicious grilled food is good for morning, noon and night, so let’s Brunch.

programme.title: Fun'Q with Spencer Watts
programme.desc: Spencer’s feeling spicy! He makes a light chickpea salad with charred halloumi and a cumin vinaigrette.

programme.title: Spencer's Big 30
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts makes a full, family-style Indian meal in around 30 minutes.

programme.title: Spencer's Big 30
programme.desc: Why order take-out when you can make it fresher, faster, and even healthier at home?

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: In our search for life beyond Earth, seeking water on other planetary bodies is an important first step.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: The James Webb Space Telescope will be our key to understanding where we fit in our enormous universe.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: Time and space converge in black holes. Now we can peer into its abyss to discover its secrets.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: Earth's new generation of orbiting telescopes promises to expand our universe of knowledge about our origins.

programme.title: Hurricane: The Anatomy
programme.desc: This episode explores the search for hurricane scientists' holy grail.

programme.title: Hurricane: The Anatomy
programme.desc: This episode looks at how plants and animals cope with the annual onslaught in the hurricane zone.

programme.title: Hurricane: The Anatomy
programme.desc: With coastal habitats and natural defenses irretrievably damaged, the future appears bleak.

programme.title: Life in Outer Space
programme.desc: Scientists are exploring planets and moons that may once have supported life. Saturn's moon, Europa, is one.

programme.title: Hubble: Eye in the Sky
programme.desc: In this beautiful show, viewers get an inside look at the challenges of operating the Hubble telescope.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: We're looking hard, but the numbers aren't stacking up. Is life on other planets really that hard to find?

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: Two new space probes can now orbit closer to the Sun than ever before, enabling groundbreaking research.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: Scientists are studying the benefits of a hybrid moon mission, one where humans and robots work in tandem.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: Climate change is causing increasing havoc to our environment. How can science respond to this challenge?

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: Venus may be considered Earth's evil twin. Hostile and unforgiving, Venus is the subject of intense scrutiny.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: The International Space Station is now the only manned station in space, but soon there may be more.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: Asteroids are everywhere in space. We count them, examine why they exist, and track them for Earth's safety.

programme.title: Voyage of Darwin's Beagle: On The Future of Species
programme.desc: About 180 years after Darwin, his great-granddaughter follows his path, traveling from Tenerife to the Amazon.

programme.title: Voyage of Darwin's Beagle: On The Future of Species
programme.sub-title: Low Life on the Ladder of God
programme.desc: Follow Darwin's journey to see remnants of slavery and to relive his discovery of fossils of extinct animals.

programme.title: Expedition Antarctica
programme.desc: A hardy research crew seeks clues about the fate of our planet beneath the icy waters of the Antarctic Ocean.

programme.title: The Jupiter Enigma
programme.desc: NASA’s Juno mission to Jupiter is revealing the origins of our solar system, when Earth's fate was uncertain.

programme.title: The Saturn V Story
programme.desc: This is the story of the most powerful machine ever built and a nation who watched on with hope, together.

programme.title: Mars Calling: Manifest Destiny or Grand Illusion
programme.desc: This film merges the mythic and the scientific to show that Mars may not be what we imagine.

programme.title: The Next Great Extinction Event
programme.desc: There have been five major extinction events in our planet's history, are we about to experience the 6th?

programme.title: Faster Than Light: the Dream of Interstellar Flight
programme.desc: Next-generation rocket technology is fueling the dream of traveling across the void.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: In our search for life beyond Earth, seeking water on other planetary bodies is an important first step.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: The James Webb Space Telescope will be our key to understanding where we fit in our enormous universe.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: Time and space converge in black holes. Now we can peer into its abyss to discover its secrets.

programme.title: Space: The New Frontier
programme.desc: Earth's new generation of orbiting telescopes promises to expand our universe of knowledge about our origins.

programme.title: Hurricane: The Anatomy
programme.desc: This episode explores the search for hurricane scientists' holy grail.

programme.title: Hurricane: The Anatomy
programme.desc: This episode looks at how plants and animals cope with the annual onslaught in the hurricane zone.

programme.title: Hurricane: The Anatomy
programme.desc: With coastal habitats and natural defenses irretrievably damaged, the future appears bleak.

programme.title: Life in Outer Space
programme.desc: Scientists are exploring planets and moons that may once have supported life. Saturn's moon, Europa, is one.

programme.title: Super Senses: Wild Feats of Evolution
programme.desc: On this episode we examine how things taste, and how vital scent is from the animal perspective.

programme.title: Super Senses: Wild Feats of Evolution
programme.desc: There are senses we share with most animals, but what about those senses that humans aren't equipped with?

programme.title: Super Senses: Wild Feats of Evolution
programme.desc: There’s a whole world of sensations that animals are aware of but humans are completely unable to detect.

programme.title: Super Senses: Wild Feats of Evolution
programme.desc: In this episode, we examine how every animal has what could be called a sense of rhythm.

programme.title: Super Senses: Wild Feats of Evolution
programme.desc: While all life began in the oceans, human beings now rely on technology to navigate the seven seas.

programme.title: Super Senses: Wild Feats of Evolution
programme.desc: While all life began in the oceans, human beings now rely on technology to navigate the seven seas.

programme.title: Super Senses: Wild Feats of Evolution
programme.desc: In this episode we find out more about the animals that live in the shadows.

programme.title: Earth's Survival: Decoding the Science
programme.desc: This documentary explains how we may be in the middle of the most crucial moment of Earth’s history.

programme.title: Fascinating Places
programme.desc: The West Coast of Madagascar is remote and inhospitable but home to an astonishing array of animals.

programme.title: Fascinating Places
programme.desc: The Northeast of Madagascar is a major trading center and one of the most species-rich regions in the world.

programme.title: Fascinating Places
programme.desc: Madagascar is an island paradise in the Indian Ocean, home to fun-loving folk and a unique natural landscape.

programme.title: Fascinating Places
programme.desc: The Black Forest captivates all who visit and has a reputation for its unique landscape and inhabitants.

programme.title: Fascinating Places
programme.sub-title: Wilderness In the Heart of Europe
programme.desc: The Vosges are home to countless species, encompassing mountains, valleys, forests, and fields.

programme.title: Fascinating Places
programme.sub-title: In the Valley of the Storks
programme.desc: The emblem of The Vosges is the stork. Once gone almost entirely, there are now 400 resident breeding pairs.

programme.title: Fascinating Places
programme.sub-title: On Safari in Kenya’s Nature Paradise
programme.desc: The Maasai Mara is one of the most breathtaking animal habitats in the world.

programme.title: Fascinating Places
programme.desc: There are few places on earth that are closer to the stars than the high Andes.

programme.title: Fascinating Places
programme.desc: Patagonia seems to be primarily uninhabited by man, which makes this harsh, wild land a paradise for animals.

programme.title: Fascinating Places
programme.sub-title: From Pantanal to the Iguacu Waterfalls
programme.desc: The Pantanal, in the southwest of Brazil, is the largest marsh in the world.

programme.title: Super Senses: Wild Feats of Evolution
programme.desc: The human imagination is limited when it comes to animal senses.

programme.title: Super Senses: Wild Feats of Evolution
programme.desc: On this episode we travel from the desert to the forest to learn how creatures never miss an auditory trick.

programme.title: Super Senses: Wild Feats of Evolution
programme.desc: On this episode of Super Senses, we get in touch with the feelings of wild animals.

programme.title: Super Senses: Wild Feats of Evolution
programme.desc: On this episode we examine how things taste, and how vital scent is from the animal perspective.

programme.title: Super Senses: Wild Feats of Evolution
programme.desc: There are senses we share with most animals, but what about those senses that humans aren't equipped with?

programme.title: Super Senses: Wild Feats of Evolution
programme.desc: There’s a whole world of sensations that animals are aware of but humans are completely unable to detect.

programme.title: Super Senses: Wild Feats of Evolution
programme.desc: In this episode, we examine how every animal has what could be called a sense of rhythm.

programme.title: Super Senses: Wild Feats of Evolution
programme.desc: While all life began in the oceans, human beings now rely on technology to navigate the seven seas.

programme.title: Super Senses: Wild Feats of Evolution
programme.desc: While all life began in the oceans, human beings now rely on technology to navigate the seven seas.

programme.title: Super Senses: Wild Feats of Evolution
programme.desc: In this episode we find out more about the animals that live in the shadows.

programme.title: Earth's Survival: Decoding the Science
programme.desc: This documentary explains how we may be in the middle of the most crucial moment of Earth’s history.

programme.title: Mega Disaster
programme.desc: Volcanoes can bury entire cities in clouds of ash and gas, or turn rivers into torrents of mud and debris.

programme.title: Mega Disaster
programme.desc: There is no stopping these massive and deadly phenomena, the only thing to do is run to higher ground.

programme.title: Mega Disaster
programme.desc: From the tremendous, to the terrifying, to the tragic, this is a countdown to a mega tornado.

programme.title: Mega Disaster
programme.desc: Earthquakes baffle experts as they try to gather the information necessary to predict the next big one.

programme.title: Earth in 1000 years
programme.desc: Earth's stores of ice on Greenland and Antarctica are melting, and sea levels rising. Where is Earth headed?

programme.title: Antarctica
programme.desc: Explore Antarctica, one of the world's most beautiful, yet inhospitable, landscapes.

programme.title: Facing Forces of Nature
programme.desc: An asteroid could be barreling toward Earth at any time, now or in the future. Only science can save us.

programme.title: Facing Forces of Nature
programme.desc: Tornadoes: Can we use modern technology to predict the emergence and path of this violent force of nature?

programme.title: Facing Forces of Nature
programme.desc: Volcanoes: How successful can we be in the fight against this violent, destructive, elemental force of nature?

programme.title: Facing Forces of Nature
programme.desc: Melting ice at Earth's poles: Do we still have a chance to stop it from melting and prevent its repercussions?

programme.title: Evolution Earth
programme.desc: From the earth to the stars to the edge of the unknown, explore the mystery, majesty and power of the world we live in and beyond.

programme.title: Evolution Earth
programme.desc: From the earth to the stars to the edge of the unknown, explore the mystery, majesty and power of the world we live in and beyond.

programme.title: Wild Moments
programme.desc: Wildlife film-makers often risk life and limb trying to capture unique images of their dangerous protagonists. In Alaska a cunning grizzly threatens the son of Andreas Kieling, on the remote Wrangel Island in northern-east Siberia his colleague Uwe Anders is taken by surprise by a a polar bear, and off the coast of South Africa a diving-team is unexpectedly surrounded by white sharks. But the life of the film-makers is also threatened by elemental forces, life-threatening diseases and failing technical equipment: Deep in the vast rainforests of West Africa cameraman Klaus Scheurich nearly dies of malaria, and in South India a film team narrowly escapes death in a hot-air balloon accident. But they also meet remarkable people such as a zoo keeper in Cameroon who feels at ease playing with half-grown and adult gorillas - the most powerful of all great apes. The first episode "Wild Moments - Dangerous and Deadly" follows wildlife film-makers on their adventurous journeys throughout the world.

programme.title: Wild Moments
programme.desc: Wildlife filmmakers often risk life and limb trying to capture unique images of their dangerous protagonists. A cunning grizzly grabs a salmon off Andreas Kieling's fishing rod, in the depths of the Amazon River a diving team has a spooky encounter with piranhas, in the Mediterranean Sea the currents nearly push Thomas Behrend into the mouths of angry sperm whales defending a newborn - and cameramen who want to film jaguars in the dense jungle of South America become easy prey for the well camouflaged cats. The life of the film-makers is also threatened by elemental forces and failing technical equipment: In the swamps of North Australia violent storms hold up the crossing of tearing rivers, a volcano in the South Seas spits burning lumps of lava into the base camp, and in Siberia a camera team breaks through the ice and has to camp at minus 30°C in the middle of nowhere. The second episode "Wild Moments - Dangerous and Deadly" follows wildlife-filmmakers on their adventurous journeys throughout the world.

programme.title: Portugal's Nature Parks
programme.desc: Madeira - Portugal's autonomous island in the Atlantic Ocean, is closer to the African coast than to the mother country Portugal, from which it is separated by about 900 kilometers. The island's name comes from Portuguese sailors who claimed the uninhabited isle for themselves in 1419. Madeira means wood and that was available in abundance. Almost the entire surface was covered by dense forest. Since 1982, two-thirds of the landscape has been protected.

programme.title: Portugal's Nature Parks
programme.desc: The "Parque Natural da Ria Formosa" is one of the largest lagoon conservation areas in Europe. Bordered by five natural islands. A mosaic of sandbanks, brackish and freshwater lagoons and salt marshes. In 2010, the "Ria Formosa" was chosen as one of the seven natural wonders of Portugal. It is a unique ecosystem: in the winter months, more than 20,000 birds gather here. In addition, the nature park has one of the largest seahorse populations internationally. More than half of all fish species in the Atlantic are said to spawn in the lagoon.

programme.title: Over the Islands of Africa
programme.sub-title: Over the Islands of Africa, Zanzibar
programme.desc: Photographer Matthias Ziegler is an Africa specialist. He has visited nearly every country on the continent on assignments for international agencies. Zanzibar, however, is new territory for him. He's come to the former slave island for a photo book – and this time he's not alone. To see the Tanzanian archipelago from the air, he has enlisted the services of ultralight pilot Richard Meredith-Hardy of England to fly him around in his FIB, or flying inflatable boat. Perfect conditions for an adventurous journey: as a world champion ultralight pilot, Richard and his FIB can take off and land practically anywhere – in theory.

programme.title: Over the Islands of Africa
programme.sub-title: Over the Islands of Africa, Mauritius
programme.desc: Renaud Van Der Meeren is a photographer and paramotor pilot. With his motorised parachute he goes on expeditions to the remotest corners on earth to photograph people and landscapes from the air. Renaud wanted to be a jet pilot, studied music at the Sorbonne, became a ski instructor, model and photographer's assistant before he found happiness in his current vocation. He especially likes to do his job in Mauritius – nowhere else does he find so many motifs as on the "sweetest pearl in the Indian Ocean". This time he's working on a photo book about Mauritius – by land, by water and in the air.

programme.title: Over the Islands of Africa
programme.sub-title: Over the Islands of Africa, Madagascar
programme.desc: Spirits, Kings, Lemurs – Madagascar treats Ami to a multitude of new impressions and spectacular pictures. Director Christian Schidlowski and his team accompanied her on her trip.

programme.title: Over the Islands of Africa
programme.sub-title: Over the Islands of Africa, Sao Tome and Principe
programme.desc: Along with photographer Rui Camilo, we travel to the West African island nation of Sao Tomé and Príncipe - in search of images that will capture the soul of this small and beautiful country.

programme.title: Over the Islands of Africa
programme.sub-title: Over the Islands of Africa, Cape Verde
programme.desc: Cape Verde – the islands of Saudade, of longing. For Stéphane and Richard it was a journey full of adventure, unforgettable encounters and magnificent photographs. Director Christian Schidlowski and his team accompanied them on their trip.

programme.title: Bosnia Herzegovina
programme.desc: Wild and unknown lies the still undiscovered natural paradise of Bosnia and Herzegovina in the heart of Europe. Rough mountains, dense forests and wild waterfalls characterize the picture of the untouched landscapes. Lynx, wolves and bears roam mountains and valleys. One of the last primeval forests in Europe is a retreat for many endangered animal species. Bosnia and Herzegovina is actually an undiscovered natural paradise in the middle of Europe.

programme.title: Bosnia Herzegovina
programme.desc: The barren, karstic south of the country, Herzegovina, also impresses with its natural landscapes. One of them is the Livno field. Not only is it the largest wetland in the country, it is also the largest regularly flooded karst field in the world. About 700 wild horses live in this unique landscape. Their ancestors were working horses that were abandoned here more than 50 years ago. Another special feature of the karst landscape are the rivers with their tufa barriers. The countless waterfalls that pour over it are a unique natural phenomenon.

programme.title: Wilderness in Europe: The Bohemian Forest
programme.desc: Pavel Hubeny, whose life would be unthinkable without the forest, is the head of the Šumava National Park. He is committed to ensuring that a primeval forest can grow for future generations, in which man does not interfere. This vision of a primeval forest is also shared by the forester Claus Bässler, who wants to counteract the extinction of species by having trees felled to create artificial deadwood and thus a new habitat for rare beetles and fungi. On the Vltava River lies the small town of Krumlov, the "Pearl of the Bohemian Forest" with its Baroque theater and fully preserved stage technology from the 17th century. Here the director Zuzana Vrbová enchants people from all over the world with her productions. Apart from agriculture, it was mainly the abundance of forests that ensured the livelihood of the inhabitants of the Bohemian Forest - including that of the carpenter Philipp von Manz, who processed local wood for his customers all over Germany. Veterinarian Judith Gollner, who grew up in the Austrian part of the Bohemian Forest, lived in Vienna for a long time and made a conscious decision to return. She likes the people and is proud of "her farmers' wives" who share her love of the Bohemian Forest.

programme.title: Wilderness in Europe: The Bohemian Forest
programme.sub-title: The Psychogramm of a Spiritual Landscape
programme.desc: The Bohemian Forest is the source of the Vltava River, home to primeval forests, untouched high moors and bubbling torrents. This archaic stretch of land, which includes the Bavarian Forest, was long considered impenetrable and mysterious. Since the fall of the Iron Curtain, Bavarian border residents have rediscovered new references to this unusual forest landscape for themselves. This is also the case with photo artist Bastian Kalous, who has been documenting his homeland for years with nostalgic-looking Polaroid photographs and is dealing with its eventful history for an illustrated book. Or Elisa Belotti and Marco Heurich, who have dedicated their lives to the lynxes. In order for Europe's largest wild cat to survive in the Bohemian Forest, the lynx researchers fight for every single animal. Iryna Rudenko came to Zwiesel from Ukraine out of a passion for glass as a material to study at the glass school. After graduation, she would like to stay in Bavaria, Anna Hones grew up in Horská Kvilda, the smallest and highest village in the Czech Republic. The village lies just under 1100 meters above sea level, directly on the former border. The farmer, who experienced communism, the fall of the Iron Curtain and the subsequent changes, is now trying to find ways of surviving in this barren region with her children.

programme.title: Portugal's Nature Parks
programme.desc: The Parque Nacional Peneda-Gerês is the only national park in Portugal. An area of almost 700 square kilometers, located in the extreme north of Portugal. Barren and rocky ridges in the plateaus, contrast with large forests and lush ground vegetation in deep valleys, crossed by lakes and rivers. The park is known for its oak forests, which are predominantly pedunculate and Pyrenean oaks. Among the typical animals of the park are the small Garrano ponies, a very old breed of horse and the only one in Europe that still lives in the wild.

programme.title: Portugal's Nature Parks
programme.desc: The highest mountain range in Portugal is the Serra da Estrela. The episode embarks on a voyage of discovery to adventurous rock formations and villages steeped in tradition. The nature park is the largest protected area on the Iberian peninsula. In the mountain range we accompany the young scientist Ana Matos, who wants to breathe new life into old traditions, and cheese maker Maria Natália Lopes. She watches over more than 350 sheeps.

programme.title: Portugal's Nature Parks
programme.sub-title: South West Portugal: Alentejano and Costa Vicentina
programme.desc: In the southwest of Portugal lies the little known Parque Natural do Sudoeste Alentejano e Costa Vicentina. It encompasses a narrow coastal strip that runs along the coast to the south of Portugal. Sparsely populated, this coastal region, which was placed under protection in 1988, is considered one of the last intact ecosystems in Europe. The steep cliffs, the almost constant wind and high waves have ensured that mass tourism has not gained a foothold here. There are still near-natural forests and a diverse bird life.

programme.title: Portugal's Nature Parks
programme.desc: Madeira - Portugal's autonomous island in the Atlantic Ocean, is closer to the African coast than to the mother country Portugal, from which it is separated by about 900 kilometers. The island's name comes from Portuguese sailors who claimed the uninhabited isle for themselves in 1419. Madeira means wood and that was available in abundance. Almost the entire surface was covered by dense forest. Since 1982, two-thirds of the landscape has been protected.

programme.title: Portugal's Nature Parks
programme.desc: The "Parque Natural da Ria Formosa" is one of the largest lagoon conservation areas in Europe. Bordered by five natural islands. A mosaic of sandbanks, brackish and freshwater lagoons and salt marshes. In 2010, the "Ria Formosa" was chosen as one of the seven natural wonders of Portugal. It is a unique ecosystem: in the winter months, more than 20,000 birds gather here. In addition, the nature park has one of the largest seahorse populations internationally. More than half of all fish species in the Atlantic are said to spawn in the lagoon.

programme.title: Over the Islands of Africa
programme.sub-title: Over the Islands of Africa, Zanzibar
programme.desc: Photographer Matthias Ziegler is an Africa specialist. He has visited nearly every country on the continent on assignments for international agencies. Zanzibar, however, is new territory for him. He's come to the former slave island for a photo book – and this time he's not alone. To see the Tanzanian archipelago from the air, he has enlisted the services of ultralight pilot Richard Meredith-Hardy of England to fly him around in his FIB, or flying inflatable boat. Perfect conditions for an adventurous journey: as a world champion ultralight pilot, Richard and his FIB can take off and land practically anywhere – in theory.

programme.title: Over the Islands of Africa
programme.sub-title: Over the Islands of Africa, Mauritius
programme.desc: Renaud Van Der Meeren is a photographer and paramotor pilot. With his motorised parachute he goes on expeditions to the remotest corners on earth to photograph people and landscapes from the air. Renaud wanted to be a jet pilot, studied music at the Sorbonne, became a ski instructor, model and photographer's assistant before he found happiness in his current vocation. He especially likes to do his job in Mauritius – nowhere else does he find so many motifs as on the "sweetest pearl in the Indian Ocean". This time he's working on a photo book about Mauritius – by land, by water and in the air.

programme.title: Over the Islands of Africa
programme.sub-title: Over the Islands of Africa, Madagascar
programme.desc: Spirits, Kings, Lemurs – Madagascar treats Ami to a multitude of new impressions and spectacular pictures. Director Christian Schidlowski and his team accompanied her on her trip.

programme.title: Over the Islands of Africa
programme.sub-title: Over the Islands of Africa, Sao Tome and Principe
programme.desc: Along with photographer Rui Camilo, we travel to the West African island nation of Sao Tomé and Príncipe - in search of images that will capture the soul of this small and beautiful country.

programme.title: Over the Islands of Africa
programme.sub-title: Over the Islands of Africa, Cape Verde
programme.desc: Cape Verde – the islands of Saudade, of longing. For Stéphane and Richard it was a journey full of adventure, unforgettable encounters and magnificent photographs. Director Christian Schidlowski and his team accompanied them on their trip.

programme.title: Bosnia Herzegovina
programme.desc: Wild and unknown lies the still undiscovered natural paradise of Bosnia and Herzegovina in the heart of Europe. Rough mountains, dense forests and wild waterfalls characterize the picture of the untouched landscapes. Lynx, wolves and bears roam mountains and valleys. One of the last primeval forests in Europe is a retreat for many endangered animal species. Bosnia and Herzegovina is actually an undiscovered natural paradise in the middle of Europe.

programme.title: Bosnia Herzegovina
programme.desc: The barren, karstic south of the country, Herzegovina, also impresses with its natural landscapes. One of them is the Livno field. Not only is it the largest wetland in the country, it is also the largest regularly flooded karst field in the world. About 700 wild horses live in this unique landscape. Their ancestors were working horses that were abandoned here more than 50 years ago. Another special feature of the karst landscape are the rivers with their tufa barriers. The countless waterfalls that pour over it are a unique natural phenomenon.

programme.title: World of Oceans
programme.desc: Animals need shelter, not just food, to protect themselves and their young.

programme.title: World of Oceans
programme.desc: Coral reefs in warm waters near the equator host a quarter of all marine species.

programme.title: World of Oceans
programme.desc: Sharks, often feared, have a deeper significance beyond their appearance.

programme.title: Animals Like Us
programme.desc: Mutualism explores animal cooperation for survival, turning host-parasite relationships into alliances.

programme.title: Animals Like Us
programme.desc: Playing games helps children and animals develop coordination, adaptability, and social skills.

programme.title: Sperm Whales: Dealing with the Unexpected
programme.desc: The Sperm Whale family in the Crozet Islands faces threats from humans, fishing lines, and deep-sea monsters.

programme.title: Sharks, Scavengers of the sea
programme.desc: New Caledonia's lagoon is a shark paradise, where they scavenge feathers, fins, and fur for prey.

programme.title: Grey Seal, A Journey of Survival
programme.desc: Grey seals, Europe's largest predators, face threats from declining fish, predators, and legal culls.

programme.title: Animals Like Us
programme.desc: Recent neuroscience shows animals can feel emotions like anger, sadness, and love, challenging old beliefs.

programme.title: Lioness in Exile
programme.desc: Manyari, the Queen, leaves her pride with her cubs to escape two young threats who want to kill them.

programme.title: Free Billy
programme.desc: Free Billy is a documentary about the campaign to free an Asian Bull elephant from LA Zoo.

programme.title: Endless Horizon
programme.desc: Namibia offers a chance to see Africa's wildlife, including elephants, lions, and hippos, in its savannahs.

programme.title: Our Nature: Call of the Wild
programme.desc: This film highlights the vital role of animals in our ecosystem and their treatment throughout the centuries.

programme.title: Animals Like Us
programme.desc: Studying chimpanzees & bonobos shows unique habits & traditions, deepening our understanding of animal culture

programme.title: World of Oceans
programme.desc: Animals need shelter, not just food, to protect themselves and their young.

programme.title: World of Oceans
programme.desc: Coral reefs in warm waters near the equator host a quarter of all marine species.

programme.title: To the Ends of the Earth
programme.desc: Discover East Africa’s incredible landscapes and the amazing birds that inhabit it.

programme.title: Primeval New Zealand
programme.desc: Peter Elliott reveals new information about the origins New Zealands 'evolutionary mutants'.

programme.title: Biebrza - The European Amazon
programme.desc: A unique story about Biebrza - the European Amazon

programme.title: Penguin Baywatch
programme.desc: King penguins, seals, and other sea animals face breeding challenges, including inbreeding.

programme.title: Penguin Baywatch
programme.desc: Macaroni penguins in Antarctica form strong family bonds, facing predators and playful fur seals.

programme.title: Super Senses
programme.desc: In this episode we'll explore the animal sense of sight

programme.title: Beak & Brain - Genius Birds From Down Under
programme.desc: These child prodigies of the animal kingdom have feathers and beaks - and very special skills!

programme.title: Parrot's Paradise
programme.desc: Aaron Pedersen explores the unique Australian characteristics that have facilitated the thriving of parrots

programme.title: Super Senses
programme.desc: Living in the aerial realm requires a unique set of senses

programme.title: Eagle - Queen Of The Skies
programme.desc: To explore the world of the eagle we follow the fortunes of one individual from hatching to leaving the nest.

programme.title: Storks - A Village Rooftop Saga
programme.desc: Ruhstadt, Germany, hosts Germany's largest white stork colony, despite facing damage to roads, roofs

programme.title: Circus In The Sky
programme.desc: With their acrobatic aerial plays, birds of prey make some of the most impressive appearances in the sky.

programme.title: Yellowstone Journals
programme.desc: Yellowstone's creatures thrive due to unique abilities, anatomies, and senses, especially in Spring.

programme.title: The National
programme.desc: The National is CBC's flagship nightly news program, featuring the day's top stories with in-depth journalism.

programme.title: CBC News NL
programme.desc: Bringing you breaking news, in-depth reports and weather you can count on.

programme.title: CBC News NL
programme.desc: Bringing you breaking news, in-depth reports and weather you can count on.

programme.title: The National
programme.desc: The National is CBC's flagship nightly news program, featuring the day's top stories with in-depth journalism.

programme.title: CBC News NL
programme.desc: Bringing you breaking news, in-depth reports and weather you can count on.

programme.title: CBC News NL

programme.desc: The National is CBC's flagship nightly news program, featuring the day's top stories with in-depth journalism.

programme.title: CBC News NL
programme.desc: Bringing you breaking news, in-depth reports and weather you can count on.

programme.title: CBC News NL

programme.title: CBC News NL

programme.title: Force of Nature Rewind
programme.desc: Wild and viral weather footage from across Canada and around the world

programme.title: Force of Nature Rewind
programme.desc: Wild and viral weather footage from across Canada and around the world

programme.title: Survivorman
programme.desc: Les feels totally trapped, immobilized by a simulated splintered leg stranded in the dense mountainous terrain

programme.title: What If
programme.desc: Take an epic journey through hypothetical worlds and possibilities.

programme.title: Crazy Creatures
programme.desc: Evolution has crafted some of the strangest beings you can imagine, and it's all been done for a reason.

programme.title: Survivorman: Bigfoot
programme.desc: Les goes to a secret location outside of Portland, Oregon with filmmaker Devon Massyn in search of Bigfoot

programme.title: Dream In Colour
programme.desc: Get lost in the breathtaking beauty of Canadian and international landscapes

programme.title: Dream In Colour
programme.desc: Get lost in the breathtaking beauty of Canadian and international landscapes

programme.title: Dream In Colour
programme.desc: Get lost in the breathtaking beauty of Canadian and international landscapes

programme.title: Destination Wild: Wild Islands
programme.desc: The Falkland Islands' pristine coastlines and bountiful seas offer a lifeline and breeding centre for millions of penguins each year.

programme.title: Nature's Cutest
programme.desc: As we all know the natural world can be tough and challenging at times, but it can also be nothing short of adorable.

programme.title: Manatees on the Move
programme.desc: Manatees, sea cows, these gentle giants go by several names. They were once thought to be beautiful mermaids that could lure sailors to their deaths, though they probably won't win too many beauty contests. To retain their weight, manatees need to eat for 8 hours and consume 8% of their bodyweight every day. And keeping eating also means keeping moving! Thanks to their tolerance of human visitors, manatees have mates everywhere, working tirelessly to ensure they roam the world for generations to come.

programme.title: Frontier Vets
programme.desc: The vet students meet for the first time and travel 7 hours to the remote clinic. We learn about their fears and insecurities -- fear of failure and the weight of responsibilities ahead.

programme.title: Frontier Vets
programme.desc: The vets get situated in their dormitory accommodations. Dr. Greg deliberately pairs them according to their differences. Vets need to learn to work with colleagues and clients different from themselves.

programme.title: Colours of Nature
programme.desc: From subtle to shocking, colour enhances the beauty of the natural world. Colour is a language to most living things, whether as a warning, camouflage or a come on. But there's more to colour than just pigment. Colours define how many animals behave, how they mate and how they inter-act with one another. For us the beautiful and varied shades of the animal kingdom form a spec-tacular kaleidoscope of colours. With all the diversity in the world, sit back and enjoy all the col-ours of the animal kingdom.

programme.title: Wild Madagascar
programme.desc: Breaking off from mainland Africa 160 million years ago, refugee animals cast adrift on natural rafts arrived on the tropical shores of Madagascar.

programme.title: Extinction! Going, Going, Gone
programme.desc: Extinction, a daunting and confusing word. A force of nature, that sees the end of species, but also heralds the birth of others. Right now the extinction rate could be as much as 10,000 times faster than it should be. 20% of fish, a fifth of all reptiles and a quarter of mammals face extinction. If we are to do anything to stem the tide of extinction we must understand the lifestyles of those in danger. The future for countless animals is uncertain, but one thing is for sure – we have a lot to learn.

programme.title: History of Life on Earth Journey To The Land
programme.desc: As the first landmasses emerged from prehistoric seas, animals took their first steps on dry land. The explosion of life that followed changed the world forever.

programme.title: Serial Killer Earth
programme.desc: Mother Nature unleashes a barrage of attacks around the globe.

programme.title: Serial Killer Earth
programme.desc: In Southern California, raging wildfires set the stage for a massive mudslide.

programme.title: Serial Killer Earth
programme.desc: Dust storms choke cities volcano menaces Pacific Northwest.

programme.title: Serial Killer Earth
programme.desc: In this episode, a flaming tornado terrorizes a team of firefighters.

programme.title: Serial Killer Earth
programme.desc: Landslide chases workers, tornado strikes airport, quake threatens old church.

programme.title: Serial Killer Earth
programme.desc: Mother Nature unleashes a swarm of tornados on a group of storm chasers.

programme.title: Serial Killer Earth
programme.desc: Mother Nature's worst hurricane, tornado, and Haiti earthquake wreak havoc.

programme.title: Serial Killer Earth
programme.desc: Mother Nature unleashes a barrage of attacks around the globe.
